---
name: Objective
about: A direction in play — the what+why we're pursuing, never the how
title: ''
labels: objective
---

<!-- An objective states the WHAT and WHY, not the HOW. The how is disposable and lives
     in "Current approach" below — it is expected to change as understanding improves, so
     it is never a commitment. Open = a direction currently in play; closed = achieved,
     abandoned, or subsumed into another objective. A standing objective (no terminal
     "done" state) may legitimately stay open. See CLAUDE.md. -->

<!-- One line: the objective as a what+why. e.g. "Give step 3 a channel that sees ensemble
     shifts over time" — not "run a 2 ns pilot". -->

## Why this matters

<!-- Compulsory. One or two sentences on what gap this closes for the project — tie back to
     the three-step pipeline in README.md, or to a named decision / result / sibling
     objective. The why is durable even when the how changes. Keep concise. -->

## How it fits

<!-- Which of the 3 steps (generate / score / modulate) this serves, and what it unlocks. -->

## Current approach — disposable; the *how*, expected to change

<!-- The method we're acting on NOW. Rewrite this freely as we learn — it is not a
     commitment and carries no audit trail. If the approach changes, overwrite it; don't
     append "update:" notes. -->

## What would make this wrong

<!-- Absorbed risks / limitations this objective must beat (the old "caveat" content, when
     it is really a risk to THIS objective rather than a standalone disclosure). Reading
     guidance for any output the objective produces. Omit if none. -->

## Blocked-by / unblocks

<!-- `Blocked-by #N` lines make the dependency graph explicit — the cross-cutting
     "what's next" is DERIVED from this (open objectives with no open blocker), never
     hand-authored. List what this unblocks too. Omit if none. -->

## Plain English

<!-- 2-4 sentences for a reader outside the project. What we're trying to achieve and why
     it's worth it — not the current mechanics. No jargon, no acronyms. -->
